questions.question_text: You have seen children crossing the carriageway and have reduced speed to 30 km/h. What do you do?
You are driving at about 50 km/h. What do you do?
You are about to catch up with the slowly moving motorcyclist at about 40 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You want to turn right at this intersection. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at about 80 km/h. What do you do?
You are driving at about 50 km/h and need to continue straight ahead. What do you do?
You are driving slowly because you are looking for a particular road. What do you do now?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
You have taken the wrong road and want to turn around as soon as possible. What do you do?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
The oncoming lorry has just appeared. You are driving at 40 to 50 km/h. What do you do?
You are driving at about 40 km/h and need to pass the stationary car on your right. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at about 70 km/h, the same speed as the vehicle ahead. What do you do?
You are about to catch up with the red car, which is slowing down. You must continue straight ahead at the intersection. What do you do?
You must continue straight ahead and have just caught up with the braking motorcyclist. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You have caught up with the slowly moving driver ahead. What do you do?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
You are driving at about 60 km/h and want to enter the motorway. There is no traffic behind you on the motorway itself. What do you do?
You have got out of the black car. Which rule applies here?

answers.1: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are prohibited here.

questions.question_text: When may you use fog lights on your car?
You are driving on a one-way road and want to turn left at the intersection. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at 40 km/h and are going straight ahead at the intersection. What do you do?
You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.1: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.

questions.question_text: You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.1: I may leave the car here for up to 3 minutes.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are prohibited here.
